description: Scattered clusters of organic moss patches and branching crack networks on a fully transparent background, for overlay onto a weathered metallic circuitry floor tile. 8-14 irregular moss blobs of varying size (small speckled 50-150px, medium 200-400px, 1-2 large 500-700px) in desaturated greens, scattered asymmetrically with denser clusters near corners and seams. 4-8 branching crack clusters ranging from short hairline fractures to long 800-1200px branching cracks, with dark near-black interiors and a faint lighter highlight edge on one side suggesting broken metal catching light. Some cracks intersect moss patches to suggest moss growing out of damage. Majority of canvas remains empty/transparent, edges fade to sparse detail to avoid a hard rectangular seam.
style: painterly stylized decay, muted desaturated palette, moss green #4A5D3A, olive #5C6B3F, dark shadow green #33421F, crack colors near-black #1A1A1A to dark brown-grey #2B2723 with highlight edge #6B655C, soft feathered organic edges on moss, sharp thin branching crack lines, naturalistic irregular random distribution, sci-fi abandoned facility reclaimed by nature aesthetic, non-cartoonish, weathered brutalist-decay mood
